rulings.Case Title: HEZZACK DANIELS VS. RECOLOGY INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Renewed Motion For Class Certification;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 15, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS HEZZACK DANIELS, TIMON HOWARD, DARRELL HUNTER, JAMES JOHNSON, HOPE DORTON, ROBERT DORTON, SHANIEQUA DORTON, A'BLAZA DORTON'S Notice Of Renewed Motion For Class Certification Granted in part. Two classes are certified. The first certified class is represented by Mr. Daniels and consists of all non-DOT regulated employees who have held the job title of classifier at the Pier 96 facility at anytime from November 1, 2009 to the present and for at least some portion of their employment during that time period they were members of Local 350. The second certified class is represented by Mr. Hunter and Mr. Smith and consists of all non-DOT regulated employees who have held the job title of material handler at the 501 Tunnel Avenue facility at anytime from November 1, 2009 to the present and for at least some portion of their employment during that time period they were members of Local 350. Both certified classes are certified only for the following claims alleged in the first amended complaint: 1) the fourth cause of action for violation of section 17200; 2) the portion of the seventh cause of action alleging invasion of privacy (but not discrimination) for violation of the California Constitution and the San Francisco Police Code; 3) the ninth cause of action for violation of the FEHA, the California Constitution and the San Francisco Police Code; 4) the thirteenth cause of action for violation of the Bane Act; and 5) the fourteenth cause of action for declaratory and injunctive relief. All other portions of the motion are denied. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.= (302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: R. R. VS. EL TRABAJO INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 15, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT VLADIMIR ABRAMOV'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead facts supporting Plaintiff's alter ego allegation. The Court disregards all of Mr. Abramov's allegations of facts not set forth in the complaint, and he is incorrect in arguing that Plaintiff must produce evidence or documents at this stage of the case. Plaintiff is allowed to plead facts on the basis of information and belief. That said, however, Plaintiff's alter ego allegations in the first amended complaint (which Mr. Abramov should have submitted with his moving papers, SFLR 2.6(C)) boil down to a legal conclusion. It is not sufficient that defendant is an officer or managing member. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: NIXON PEABODY LLP VS. GREG SHENKMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 15, 2013, Line 9, PLAINTIFF NIXON PEABODY LLP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment on its complaint is denied. There is at least a triable issue as to the effect on the enforceability and terms of the contract arising from the alleged promise/misrepresentation that allegedly induced Defendant to enter into the contract as well as an issue as to the reasonable amount of fees. Even assuming that the base contract is enforceable, Defendant would be entitled to contest the amount of fees Plaintiff could collect. Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment on the cross-complaint is denied. There is at least a triable issue as to the existence of a negligent misrepresentation and whether any reliance on it was justified. The motion for summary adjudication is additionally denied for failure to comply with the mandatory requirements of CRC 3.1350 concerning the form of the separate statement, which cannot be corrected by amended papers submitted with the reply. The objection to the use of Defendant's earlier declaration is overruled. Admissible evidence is not made inadmissible simply because it was also submitted to the arbitrator. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which is consistent with the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and compliant with CCP section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 15, 2013, Line 24, CROSS DEFENDANT ZYNGA INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT Continued to March 15, 2013 to allow moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of the motion to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. The Court has a courtesy copy of the opposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: IRENE HSIEH VS. SOLAR POWER INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 15, 2013, Line 25, DEFENDANTS SOLAR POWER INC., STEPEHN KIRCHER, JAMES PEKARSKY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Granted as to the prayer for attorneys fees. Otherwise, off calendar in light of the ruling on the demurrer, with the expectation that any amended complaint will not include allegations without any relevance to the claims stated in that amended pleading, and without prejudice to the motion to strike being renewed if appropriate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: IRENE HSIEH VS. SOLAR POWER INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 15, 2013, Line 25, DEFENDANTS SOLAR POWER INC., STEPEHN KIRCHER, JAMES PEKARSKY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend as to the retaliation claim against the individual defendants. Otherwise, sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead facts and a viable legal theory of actionable retaliation and to plead facts sufficient to support a defamation claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)
